step_by_step_thinking:
Agree, based on the rationale provided that direct confirmation via imaging provides definitive verification of the ICD's location which cannot be achieved solely from observation and auscultation. Additionally, repeated x-rays serve as checks over time ensuring ongoing correctness post-placement.
```
Agree. The addition of X-ray imaging ensures precise localization of the ICD tip, confirming it is optimally positioned relative to the lung mechanics—a critical aspect overlooked by merely relying on visual inspections and listening for airflow sounds. Repeatedly taking X-rays also offers reassurance throughout treatment, particularly after positional shifts due to movement or manipulation.
